## SK Hynix US Debut Rises ~14% as Memory Demand Narrative Drives Wall Street Interest

A high-profile US debut for SK Hynix creates a new liquid proxy for HBM memory exposure, directly sharpening the relative trade versus Micron; PM should assess whether institutional flows into the ADR represent incremental memory allocation or rotation out of MU.

- SK Hynix shares rose approximately 14% on their Wall Street debut per this outlet; Forbes coverage of the same event reports ~17% — exact figure varies across sources.
- Coverage frames the move as driven by soaring demand for memory chips.
- The story positions SK Hynix explicitly as a Micron competitor, sharpening the relative comparison between the two names.
- Strong debut signals meaningful US investor appetite for memory cycle exposure.
